items.extracted_item_name: CHETAK NY - TROUBLESHOOT HEATING UNIT / MAINTENANCE 2 ROOF TOP UNITS
items.match_reason: Step 1: This invoice item describes a service call — troubleshooting a heating unit and performing maintenance on 2 rooftop HVAC units at a location called 'CHETAK NY'. This is clearly a labor/service charge, not a physical product. Category: service. Step 2: Catalog has two entries: 'CONTRACT SERVICE' (ID: 50446, $928.43) — a broad service agreement or service labor charge, covering maintenance/repair work performed under contract; and 'INFRARED CEILING HEATER WITH PARTS' (ID: 224614, $5396.00) — a physical product (heater unit with parts), not a service. Step 3: The invoice item is a field service charge for troubleshooting and maintenance of rooftop heating units. 'CONTRACT SERVICE' maps well to this — an HVAC supplier offering contract-based maintenance and repair labor is standard. The decoded meanings align: invoice = service labor, catalog = service contract. 'INFRARED CEILING HEATER WITH PARTS' is eliminated as it is a physical product purchase, not a service call. The price difference ($672 invoice vs $928.43 catalog) is moderate but not disqualifying for a service item billed at a variable rate. Step 4: This is a clear categorical match. CONTRACT SERVICE is the logical coding for a maintenance/troubleshooting service call from an HVAC supplier.
